---
title: "Untitled"
format: html
---

```{r}
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Updated 3-panel visualization:
# p1 = raw data
# p2 = basis contributions (colored) + their SUM (smooth-only, red)
# p3 = raw data + full fitted mean (black) with contributions shown as
#      intercept + cumulative partial sums (faint gray) on the SAME response scale
# ------------------------------------------------------------

library(mgcv)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(patchwork)

set.seed(42)

# 1) Simulate irregular data (NO sin/cos)
n <- 220
x <- sort(runif(n, 0, 10))

f_true <- function(x) {
  0.25 * x - 0.02 * x^2 +
    ifelse(x > 2.5,  1.0 * (x - 2.5), 0) +
    ifelse(x > 6.0, -1.4 * (x - 6.0), 0) +
    1.8 * exp(-0.5 * ((x - 8.3)/0.55)^2) -
    1.6 * exp(-0.5 * ((x - 4.8)/0.70)^2)
}

y <- f_true(x) + rnorm(n, sd = 0.7)
dat <- data.frame(x, y)

# 2) Fit GAM
m <- gam(y ~ s(x, bs = "cr", k = 8),
         data = dat,
         method = "REML")

# 3) Grid + lpmatrix
xgrid <- data.frame(x = seq(min(dat$x), max(dat$x), length.out = 400))
Xp <- predict(m, newdata = xgrid, type = "lpmatrix")

# Smooth columns
ind <- m$smooth[[1]]$first.para:m$smooth[[1]]$last.para
B <- Xp[, ind, drop = FALSE]
b <- coef(m)[ind]

# Intercept
b0 <- coef(m)[1]

# Basis contributions matrix: beta_k * b_k(x)
Cmat <- sweep(B, 2, b, `*`)  # n_grid x n_basis

# Smooth-only sum (no intercept): sum_k beta_k * b_k(x)
smooth_only <- rowSums(Cmat)
smooth_df <- data.frame(x = xgrid$x, smooth_only = smooth_only)

# Full fitted mean on response scale: intercept + smooth_only
fit_full <- b0 + smooth_only
fit_df <- data.frame(x = xgrid$x, fit_full = fit_full, intercept = b0)

# Long format contributions for plotting
contrib_df <- as.data.frame(Cmat)
contrib_df$x <- xgrid$x

contrib_long <- contrib_df %>%
  pivot_longer(-x, names_to = "component", values_to = "contrib") %>%
  mutate(component = factor(component, levels = unique(component)))

# Cumulative partial fits on response scale: intercept + cumsum(contributions)
partial_mat <- apply(Cmat, 1, cumsum)   # n_basis x n_grid
partial_mat <- t(partial_mat) + b0      # n_grid x n_basis, on response scale

partial_df <- as.data.frame(partial_mat)
partial_df$x <- xgrid$x

partial_long <- partial_df %>%
  pivot_longer(-x, names_to = "step", values_to = "partial_fit")

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# p1: Raw data
# ------------------------------------------------------------
p1 <- ggplot(dat, aes(x, y)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.65, color = "steelblue") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "1) Raw data",
       x = "x", y = "y")

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# p2: Basis contributions + their sum (smooth-only)
# ------------------------------------------------------------
p2 <- ggplot() +
  geom_line(data = contrib_long,
            aes(x, contrib, color = component, group = component),
            linewidth = 1, alpha = 0.95) +
  geom_line(data = smooth_df,
            aes(x, smooth_only),
            linewidth = 1.6, color = "firebrick") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_color_viridis_d(end = 0.95) +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  labs(title = "2) Basis contributions add up to the smooth",
       subtitle = "Colored = β_k · b_k(x); Red = sum of colored lines (smooth-only, no intercept)",
       x = "x", y = "Contribution / smooth-only")

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# p3: Raw data + full fitted mean + (optional) partial-build gray lines
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# p3: Raw data + fitted curve + faint basis contributions
# ------------------------------------------------------------

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# p3: Contributions + their simple sum (smooth-only)
# ------------------------------------------------------------

p3 <- ggplot() +
  
  # subtle colored contribution curves
  geom_line(data = contrib_long,
            aes(x, contrib, color = component, group = component),
            linewidth = 1,
            alpha = 0.20) +
  
  # simple sum of contributions (smooth-only)
  geom_line(data = smooth_df,
            aes(x, smooth_only),
            linewidth = 1.8,
            color = "firebrick") +
  
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_color_viridis_d(end = 0.95) +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  
  labs(title = "Basis contributions and their sum",
       subtitle = "Faint colored lines = β_k · b_k(x); Red = sum of those lines (smooth-only)",
       x = "x",
       y = "Smooth term value")


```
